data.title: Quick Start
data.description: Scaffold a new project and have a CMS-powered site running in under 5 minutes.

sections.headline: Up and running in under 5 minutes.
sections.subline: One command to scaffold. Install. Build. Deploy. That's it.

sections.heading: 1. Create a new project
sections.description: The scaffolder creates a complete project with schema, example content, and AI configuration.
sections.code:
$ npx @webhouse/cms init my-site

my-site/
  cms.config.ts          # Collection + field definitions
  package.json           # Dependencies
  .env                   # AI provider keys
  content/
    posts/
      hello-world.json   # Example document

sections.heading: 2. Install and run
sections.description: Start the dev server and admin UI. Content changes are live-reloaded.
sections.code:
$ cd my-site
$ npm install
$ npx cms dev

✓ Admin UI → http://localhost:3000/admin
✓ Dev server → http://localhost:3000
✓ Watching for changes...

sections.heading: 3. Build for production
sections.description: The build pipeline generates optimized static HTML with SEO, sitemaps, RSS, and AI discovery files.
sections.code:
$ npx cms build

✓ 12 pages rendered
✓ sitemap.xml generated
✓ robots.txt generated
✓ llms.txt + llms-full.txt generated
✓ feed.xml RSS feed generated
✓ Per-page .md files for AI
→ Build complete in 1.2s

sections.heading: What you get
sections.body:
Every build generates a complete static site with:

- **HTML pages** for all published documents
- **sitemap.xml** for search engines (with hreflang for i18n)
- **robots.txt** with AI-optimized crawler rules
- **llms.txt** and **llms-full.txt** for AI discovery
- **feed.xml** RSS feed
- **Per-page .md files** for AI consumption
- **JSON-LD** structured data on every page
- **OG metadata** for social sharing

sections.heading: Next steps
sections.subheading: Define your own collections. Add AI agents. Deploy to production.
